title: Feature request: ONBUILD for oci images
body:
**Description**
>  A mechanism to support something similar to ONBUILD instructions provided by docker. For the actual functionality, I'm looking for a standard way to imprint the "toolchain" for images built by my utility. I want this stored both as a label as well as an ENV and tracks the image layers all the way back to scratch. 

---
image1.Dockerfiile
```
FROM <scratch>
LABEL $NAMESPACE.genesis="scratch"
....
ONBUILD ARG GENESIS="<image1url> (<git commit>); scratch"
```

image2.Dockerfile
```
FROM <^^>
....
ONBUILD ARG GENESIS="<image2url> (<git commit>);${GENESIS}"
ONBUILD ENV GENESIS="$GENESIS"
ONBUILD LABEL $NAMESPACE.genesis="$GENESIS"
```

I'd like to be able to pull these labels with `buildah inspect <image uri>|jq '.OCIv1.config.Labels."$NAMESPACE.genesis"'` in order to see the full list of layers and versions. I'm completely open to better ways to do this.

comments.body:
OCI Images do not support ONBUILD, so would need to build Docker image.

Do you expect buildah to help generate some of these fields?

1) Validating that this functionality doesn't already exist elsewhere and I simply missed it
2) Verifying that this is a valid thing to do

I'd prefer to omit the --format=docker when I run these. Is there a workaround that I could use for oci images?

I've build in an incredibly ugly workaround by explicitly passing the first FROM statement in a grep of the build file to a json decode function and then the result to --build-arg GENESIS=. 

I'm still very curious how others are solving this problem. Is there anything within buildah that would expose values for this? I'd be much happier if I could use .FromImage rather than looking at the file beforehand and populating a build-arg, or if there were instructions I could inject in the bud process prior to or after commit. Config only works with active containers, not images correct?

You can set
export BUILDAH_FORMAT=docker

And this will get it to default to the docker format.

```
man buildah bud
...
       --format

       Control the format for the built image's manifest and configuration data.  Recognized formats include oci (OCI image-spec v1.0, the default) and docker (version 2, using schema format 2 for the man‐
       ifest).

       Note: You can also override the default format by setting the BUILDAH_FORMAT environment variable.  export BUILDAH_FORMAT=docker
```
I know. I stated that I was specifically avoiding this in an effort to retain oci images.
@kbaegis Join me (dwalsh) on #buildah on IRC on Freenode
This would need to be added to the spec.  So open the issue there.
